title: Assessing the Impact of Racial Disparities on 30-Day Emergency Department Visits Due to Hysterectomy Complications: A Multi-State Retrospective Cohort Study
venue: International Journal of Gynaecology and Obstetrics
abstract: A multi-state retrospective cohort study evaluating the impact of racial disparities on 30-day emergency department utilization following hysterectomy complications, using HCUP databases covering approximately 200,000 hysterectomy cases. Multivariable logistic regression and sensitivity analysis identify systematic differences in ED utilization across racial groups.

title: Synergy of Diagnosis Coding Between Administrative Claims and Electronic Health Records of Large Patient Populations Across Multiple Healthcare Organizations
abstract: Analysis of diagnostic concordance and discrepancies between administrative claims and EHR systems across 17 million patients at 56 healthcare organizations, using Optum Labs data. Evaluates systematic gaps in diagnosis coding and develops large-scale data quality assessment pipelines for real-world healthcare data.

title: A Multidimensional Hierarchical Framework for Sources of Bias in Real-World Healthcare Evidence: A Scoping Review
abstract: A scoping review developing a multidimensional hierarchical framework for understanding and classifying sources of bias in real-world clinical EHR data. The framework provides structured vocabulary for bias identification and mitigation in observational health research.

title: Hypertensive Disorders of Pregnancy and Premature Cardiovascular Disease in a Diverse Cohort of Young US Women
abstract: Analysis of hypertensive disorders of pregnancy and their association with premature cardiovascular disease in a diverse cohort of young US women. Uses large-scale real-world healthcare data to characterize long-term cardiovascular risk following pregnancy complications.

title: Large Language Models in Critical Care Medicine: A Scoping Review
abstract: A scoping review synthesizing the current state of large language model applications in critical care medicine, covering clinical documentation, decision support, and real-time monitoring use cases. Evaluates evidence quality and identifies key gaps in prospective evaluation.

title: Evaluation of Large Language Model Performance in Assessing Health Economic Study Quality
venue: Journal of Health Economics and Outcomes Research
abstract: Systematic evaluation of LLM performance in assessing methodological quality of health economic studies. Benchmarks LLM outputs against expert reviewer ratings and identifies calibration gaps relevant to automated evidence synthesis pipelines.

title: Machine Learning-Based Identification and Validation of Immune-Related Biomarkers for Early Diagnosis and Targeted Therapy in Diabetic Retinopathy
abstract: Machine learning-based identification and external validation of immune-related biomarkers for early diagnosis of diabetic retinopathy, combining transcriptomic data with clinical feature analysis to identify potential therapeutic targets.

title: LLM-Based Agents for Automated Research Reproducibility in Alzheimer's Disease
venue: Journal of Medical Internet Research (Under Review)
abstract: Co-first authored study developing and evaluating LLM-based agent pipelines for automating and improving reproducibility of research workflows in Alzheimer's disease literature, with implications for systematic review automation and evidence synthesis infrastructure.

title: AgentDose: Toward Accurate and Scalable Steroid Dose Extraction in OMOP Using NLP Parsers and LLM Agents
venue: OHDSI Global Symposium 2025 (Nominee, Best Community Contribution Showcase)
abstract: Presents AgentDose, a hybrid pipeline combining rule-based NLP parsers with LLM agents for extracting corticosteroid dosing instructions from OMOP CDM prescription signatures at scale. Enables reproducible steroid exposure estimation across distributed OHDSI partner databases.

title: Racial Disparities in 30-Day ED Visits Due to Hysterectomy Complications
venue: AMIA Annual Symposium 2025 (Podium; Selected for Recording & On-Demand Dissemination)
abstract: Podium presentation examining racial disparities in 30-day emergency department utilization following hysterectomy complications, selected for recording and on-demand dissemination at AMIA Annual Symposium 2025.

title: Reinforcement Learning-Based Decision-Making for Renal Replacement Therapy in ICU-Acquired AKI Patients
venue: ACM KDD 2024 Workshop on AI for Decision Support in Healthcare
abstract: Reinforcement learning framework for optimizing renal replacement therapy decisions in ICU patients with acute kidney injury, evaluated on real-world EHR data. Demonstrates improved decision policy performance relative to standard of care under clinical outcome metrics.

title: Soft Vector Quantization for Non-Parallel Any-to-Many Voice Conversion
venue: Speech and Language Processing Technology Conference
abstract: SoftVQ: a vector quantization framework for non-parallel many-to-many voice conversion, achieving improvements of 76.3 points in perceived audio quality and 1.1 points in speaker similarity in ABX evaluations. Built as part of work at the Human Language Technology Laboratory, CUHK Shenzhen.
